Functional Modules.Module Name: High-Efficiency Inductive Transformation and Precision Regulation Module
Functional Modules.Function Description: Stabilizes low-potential energy into a constant high-voltage rail using a closed-loop negative feedback network to provide a noise-free power environment for logic and sensing circuits.
Functional Modules.Signal Sources: Low-voltage DC input, feedback voltage from the output rail.
Functional Modules.Signal Destinations: Regulated high-voltage DC rail, feedback network input.
Functional Modules.Implementation Logic: Utilizes a switching converter topology (e.g., boost converter) with an inductor for energy transformation. A precision error amplifier compares a scaled-down output voltage with a stable reference to generate a control signal, which modulates the switch duty cycle via a PWM controller to maintain a constant output voltage, forming the closed-loop negative feedback.

Functional Modules.Module Name: Bi-Directional Power Bridge and Inductive Modulation Module
Functional Modules.Function Description: Acts as a logic-decoupled power gateway to drive inductive loads with fast switching transitions and integrated energy clamping for safe back-EMF mitigation.
Functional Modules.Signal Sources: Logic-level control signals, high-voltage DC rail from the regulation module, back-EMF from the inductive load.
Functional Modules.Implementation Logic: Implements an H-bridge or half-bridge circuit using MOSFETs/IGBTs with isolated gate drivers to decouple logic signals from power switching. Includes snubber circuits and/or active clamping (e.g., using diodes/TVS) across switches to safely dissipate or recycle inductive energy during switching transitions, mitigating back-EMF throughout operation.

Functional Modules.Module Name: Closed-Loop Negative Feedback and Noise Management Network
Functional Modules.Function Description: Provides stable regulation and a noise-free environment by sensing the output, comparing it to a reference, and compensating the control loop, while filtering noise from the power rail.
Functional Modules.Signal Sources: Regulated high-voltage DC rail, stable voltage reference.
Functional Modules.Signal Destinations: Control input of the inductive transformation module, filtered power rail to logic/sensing circuits.
Functional Modules.Implementation Logic: Employs a voltage divider to sense the output, an error amplifier (e.g., op-amp) for comparison with a precision reference, and a compensation network (e.g., type II/III) to ensure loop stability. Includes LC/RC filters on the output rail to attenuate switching noise, ensuring clean power for sensitive circuits.
